text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Sunday, July 5, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — The Midwest's richest board right now. **Top scale $54.75** with Meade Electric pushing **10% above scale** on the massive Meta Data Center (Lebanon). That's five 12-hour shifts Monday-Friday plus Saturday work for serious bank. Google and Eli Lilly projects add depth across 38 calls and 187 hands needed. Book isn't thin, so early birds get the premium slots.

**LU-1105 Newark, OH** — Don't sleep on 33 calls here. The Superior Group dominates with multiple Conesville projects running 4-10s and 1-8s, all OT paid double time. Mid-City's Vantage project adds 18 more slots with free lunches and consistent double-time overtime. Pure volume play with steady work.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** — 36 calls across solid industrial work. **$48 scale** with Inglett & Stubbs running 63 hands across QTS Fayette and Wellstar Hospital gigs. Miller Electrical's cable splicer call pays **$40.90** but wants 3 years documented experience—specialized work, specialized pay. 213 hands hunting means you'll need cert or skills to stand out.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Four contractors stacked on the Port Washington Data Center REPOST. **JW wage plus $10/hour bump** across 63 combined calls (MJ, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein). These are REPOSTs, which means turnover or expansion—move fast.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge play offers **$44.80 scale plus $100/day non-taxed per diem** if you're 100+ miles out. ND License gets you another $100 daily. All three Parsons calls stack to 75 hands with second-shift incentive pay bumping earnings further.

The data center gold rush continues heating up the Midwest and northern plains—premium pay, long hours, deep books.
